name: Mitochondrial biogenesis and dynamics surveillance pass for June 5, 2026

scope.question: What changed for Mitochondrial Biogenesis and Dynamics since the 2026-06-02 public baseline publication, and does any change justify a public update?

summary: Ran a delta surveillance pass from the 2026-06-02T19:00:55Z public baseline publication through 2026-06-05. PubMed date-window searches for mitochondrial biogenesis, mitochondrial dynamics, PGC-1alpha, structured exercise, elamipretide, mavodelpar, REN001, older adults, frailty, sarcopenia, and exercise returned close records, but none supplied new controlled older-human functional evidence, a druglike aging-relevant efficacy result, a posted SHAPE elamipretide result, or a safety/durability signal that changes the public outlook. ClinicalTrials.gov checks found SHAPE still recruiting with no posted results and STRIDE unchanged with posted null functional results. Updated-registry screens returned adjacent PAD, urolithin A, HFpEF, oncology, and young-adult or disease-specific records without track-moving results. A May 2026 mavodelpar phase 1 paper and an EMA discontinuation notice reinforce the current druglike-translation boundary, but both are pre-baseline context and do not alter stage, confidence, momentum, blockers, evidence maps, or activity status. No public outlook, source, study, finding, activity, bundle, or coverage assessment update is warranted.

materiality_decision.rationale: The current public baseline already assigns low-confidence human_functional_benefit through supervised exercise and explicitly limits druglike translation using metformin exercise-interaction, STRIDE mavodelpar null results, animal-only berberine, and SHAPE elamipretide activity-only context. Current PubMed, ClinicalTrials.gov, preprint, regulator, sponsor, and web checks did not identify a post-baseline result that changes those public claims.

search_log.query: (mitochondrial biogenesis OR mitochondrial dynamics OR PGC-1alpha OR PGC-1a OR elamipretide OR mavodelpar OR REN001) AND (aging OR older OR elderly OR frailty OR sarcopenia OR exercise), Entrez dates 2026-06-02 to 2026-06-05
search_log.summary: Returned eight close Entrez-date records. Reviewed records were broad exercise-myokine or sarcopenia reviews, aging-brain mitochondrial-dynamics context, aged-rat or disease-model papers, preprints, or wrong-scope disease models; none changed the public exercise-anchored or druglike-boundary interpretation.

search_log.query: (mitochondrial biogenesis OR mitochondrial dynamics OR mitochondrial respiration OR mitochondrial function) AND (older adults OR elderly OR aged OR frailty OR sarcopenia) AND (exercise OR training OR intervention OR trial), Entrez dates 2026-06-02 to 2026-06-05
search_log.summary: Returned seven records in the broad query, but a stricter title/abstract screen for exercise training, mitochondrial terms, and older/frailty/sarcopenia terms returned zero same-window records. No new older-adult exercise-mitochondrial trial result was found.

search_log.query: (elamipretide OR SS-31 OR mavodelpar OR REN001 OR PPAR-delta OR PPARdelta) AND (aging OR older OR mitochondrial myopathy OR muscle OR exercise), Entrez dates 2026-06-02 to 2026-06-05
search_log.summary: Returned zero same-window drug-specific records for elamipretide, mavodelpar, REN001, or PPAR-delta terms.

search_log.query: mavodelpar OR REN001, all dates, checked for newly visible primary sources missed by the baseline
search_log.summary: Returned PMID 42151205, a May 18, 2026 Scientific Reports phase 1b open-label mavodelpar PMM trial. It is pre-baseline context and reinforces the existing boundary: exploratory walk and fatigue signals were uncontrolled, no consistent muscle-biopsy mitochondrial-function change was detected, and the paper itself notes the later phase 2b trial did not demonstrate clinical efficacy.

SHAPE remains recruiting, phase 2, open-label, single-arm, estimated enrollment 30, adults aged 65 to 80, with no posted results. Last update posted remains 2025-12-10; the API version holder was 2026-06-05.

search_log.query: NCT04535609 STRIDE mavodelpar primary mitochondrial myopathy registry
search_log.summary: STRIDE remains completed with posted results and no current status movement. The posted 12-minute walk and fatigue outcomes remain non-supportive for a druglike functional-benefit upgrade in this track.

search_log.query: Updated records 2026-06-02 to 2026-06-05 for elamipretide, mavodelpar, REN001, mitochondrial biogenesis, mitochondrial dynamics, PGC-1alpha, aging, older adults, sarcopenia, frailty, exercise, mitochondrial myopathy, and related terms
search_log.summary: Returned updated adjacent records including PAD compression/exercise, urolithin A healthy-volunteer glucose metabolism, HFpEF nitrate/carnitine/NR, cancer exercise, and young or disease-specific mitochondrial records. None had posted results or direct mitochondrial-biogenesis/dynamics aging evidence for this track.

search_log.query: Date window 2026-06-02 to 2026-06-05 with mitochondrial biogenesis, mitochondrial dynamics, PGC, elamipretide, mavodelpar, REN001, mitochondrial respiration, older adult, sarcopenia, and frailty screens
search_log.summary: bioRxiv returned no matching title or abstract hits. medRxiv returned one mild-neurocognitive-disorder rTMS study protocol hit by older-adult terms, but it did not test mitochondrial biogenesis/dynamics or a relevant intervention.

search_log.query: Baseline support PMIDs 28273480, 23873965, 17520024, 30548390, 29806860, and close mavodelpar PMID 42151205 with retraction, erratum, or expression-of-concern publication types
search_log.summary: Returned zero correction, retraction, erratum, or expression-of-concern records for the baseline PubMed sources and the close mavodelpar phase 1 source.

search_log.source_name: Web search and primary source follow-up
search_log.query: NCT07275424 elamipretide SHAPE healthy aging results; NCT04535609 mavodelpar STRIDE results REN001; elamipretide SHAPE healthy aging pilot older adults results June 2026; mavodelpar REN001 STRIDE primary mitochondrial myopathy June 2026
search_log.summary: Returned the SHAPE ClinicalTrials.gov record, the UW SHAPE recruitment page, STRIDE ClinicalTrials.gov results, a May 2026 Scientific Reports mavodelpar phase 1 paper, and an EMA mavodelpar paediatric-development discontinuation notice. These sources did not identify post-baseline efficacy results or a public interpretation change.

excluded_sources.title: Mavodelpar in patients with primary mitochondrial myopathy: a phase 1 trial.
excluded_sources.reason: Pre-baseline May 2026 open-label PMM phase 1b paper. It reports exploratory 12-minute-walk and fatigue improvements but no consistent muscle-biopsy mitochondrial-function change and explicitly notes that the subsequent phase 2b trial did not demonstrate clinical efficacy. It reinforces, but does not change, the existing STRIDE-based druglike-boundary language.

excluded_sources.title: Notification of discontinuation of a paediatric development which is covered by an agreed PIP Decision
excluded_sources.reason: The notice states mavodelpar PMM adult development was discontinued because of lack of efficacy signal based on 2023 pivotal trial top-line data. The public baseline already captures the STRIDE lack-of-efficacy boundary; this does not add a post-baseline delta.

excluded_sources.title: A systematic review of molecular signaling in the muscle-brain-gut axis: exercise-induced myokines and microbial metabolites as key mediators.
excluded_sources.reason: Broad exercise myokine and microbial-metabolite review. It discusses mitochondrial function within exercise systems biology but does not provide a new older-adult mitochondrial-biogenesis/dynamics intervention result or alter the current exercise-specific baseline.

excluded_sources.title: Cell-specific energy crisis in the ageing brain: Mitochondrial dynamics drives neuron-glia metabolic uncoupling and neurodegeneration.
excluded_sources.reason: Aging-brain mitochondrial-dynamics context rather than an intervention or older-human functional result for the mitochondrial biogenesis and dynamics track.

excluded_sources.title: Exercise based Intervention For Metabolic Inflexibility Linked With Lipid Storage Myopathy Using Innovative CRISPR Etf-QO Mutant Knock-in Models.
excluded_sources.reason: Preprint using Drosophila lipid-storage myopathy models. It is disease-model and preclinical context, not an aging-relevant human biogenesis/dynamics result.

excluded_sources.title: Study of Healthy Aging and Physical Function With Elamipretide
excluded_sources.reason: Still recruiting with no posted results and no post-baseline status movement. It remains activity-only context.

excluded_sources.title: An Efficacy and Safety Study of 24 Week Treatment With Mavodelpar (REN001) in Primary Mitochondrial Myopathy Patients
excluded_sources.reason: Completed registry with posted results already represented in the public evidence map; no current registry delta was found.

excluded_sources.title: Intermittent Pneumatic Compression With and Without Exercise to Improve Functioning in Peripheral Artery Disease
excluded_sources.reason: Registry updated on 2026-06-04, but it tests PAD compression/exercise rather than mitochondrial biogenesis/dynamics as an aging intervention and has no posted results.

excluded_sources.title: Effects of Urolithin A Supplementation on Glucose Metabolism in Healthy Adults 55 >= Years Old: A Randomized Triple-Masked Controlled Clinical Trial
excluded_sources.reason: Recruiting healthy-volunteer urolithin A study updated on 2026-06-05 with no posted results; it is better tracked under mitophagy or metabolite-restoration contexts than this biogenesis/dynamics track.

notes: Triage selected this as the top ranked surveillance item because it is the next distinct target after recently handled surveillance and coverage-repair sessions.
No coverage assessment exists yet for this track; this ordinary surveillance pass did not discover a material category-level change requiring immediate coverage repair.

The public boundary remains unchanged: structured exercise supplies the low-confidence human functional anchor, while druglike PPAR/PGC-1alpha, cardiolipin, metformin, berberine, and elamipretide signals remain disease-specific, animal-only, null/boundary, or activity-only.
next_actions: Continue ordinary surveillance for posted SHAPE elamipretide results, new older-adult exercise-mitochondrial trials, or controlled druglike mitochondrial-biogenesis/dynamics results.
Backfill a coverage assessment as a separate maintenance task if internal source-landscape completeness is prioritized.
Create a public bundle only if future evidence changes stage, confidence, momentum, blockers, safety, durability, activity status, or evidence-map wording.
